sglang.title: [RL] Add --nccl-port to prevent port conflict
sglang.author: zhuzilin
sglang.bodyExcerpt:
Motivation
In RL training, we need to start multiple sglang server on one machine, and by default, sglang will allocate an random port to init nccl, which may result in address conflict. Therefore we propose to allocate all ports in advance by adding --nccl-port arguments.
Thank you for your time on

sglang.title: [RL] add pause and continue generation for async rl training
sglang.author: zhuzilin
sglang.bodyExcerpt:
Motivation
When doing asynchronous RL training with external agentic environment, the env will keep on sending generation request to sglang server, which will hinder weight updation. Therefore, we need to lock the generation during weight updation.
This PR is trying to implement the following asyncr

sglang.title: Optimize Hopper CUTLASS FP8 Blockwise Grouped GEMM Kernel in Small K Scenario
sglang.author: HydraQYH
sglang.bodyExcerpt:
Motivation
Follow #7278
When K in GEMM problem size is small, CUTLASS Kernel shows suboptimal performance. The reason is that when K is small, the execution time of Mainloop is very short and Epilogue is difficult to be overlapped by Mainloop. In CUTLASS, Epilogue performs unnecessary LinearCombinat

sglang.title: Embedding parallel by attn_tp
sglang.author: MoonBall
sglang.bodyExcerpt:
Motivation
When the dp-size > 1, the Embedding layer does not employ tensor parallelism (tp), requiring each tp group to load the full matrix.
Under the configuration tp-size=16, dp-size=4, and model=DeepSeek-R1:

Before modification: Each tp group’s Embedding occupied 1767.5 MiB of VRAM.
After modi

sglang.author: mickqian
sglang.bodyExcerpt:
Motivation
Some bugs in apply_shuffle_mul_sum_kernel:


some experts outputs were wrongly truncated by src_row >= m, where src_row is mistakenly taken as original
token index ( [0, m) )


in original impl, the thread-count assigned to a block is insufficient to cover row_stride(hidden_size, 4096). M

vllm.title: [Structured Outputs][V1] Skipping with models doesn't contain tokenizers
vllm.author: aarnphm
vllm.bodyExcerpt:
This PR address issues that #20072 runs into where the models doesn't provide a tokenizer. In this case, we shouldn't allow structured outputs to be used with these models.
I added check to make sure that we won't interfere with these workflows.
Signed-off-by: Aaron Pham contact@aarnphm.xyz
